Impact directions derived from channel scores and sensitivity weights. POSITIVE = BULLISH signal for US-listed stock. NEGATIVE = BEARISH signal. All signals are analytical only -- no orders generated.
Methodology & Data Sources
LBRI Composition
Weighted composite of 5 kill conditions: banking collapse (30%), Hezbollah escalation (25%), IMF programme (25%), infrastructure attack (15%), LBP FX (5%). Single-trigger override for KC_01.
US Impact Channels
4 transmission channels: Mideast geopolitical (35%), EM banking contagion (30%), CB credibility signal (20%), safe haven demand (15%). Stocks selected for Lebanon exposure specificity.
Data Coverage
LBRI history: Jan 2023 - Jul 2026 (monthly). BDL reserves: Q1 2021 onwards (quarterly). LBP parallel rate: Jan 2023 - Jul 2026 (monthly). Eurobond status: as of Jul 2026.
Signal Zones
NORMAL <0.40 WATCH 0.40-0.55 ALERT 0.55-0.65 CRITICAL >0.65

Supply Chain Risk Intelligence
Chokepoint · Freight Rate · Critical Minerals · Nearshoring · Logistics
SCRI  0.1400  NORMAL

Banque du Liban operates a frontier, structurally shallow supply chain, heavily dependent on a small number of entry points with minimal alternative routing — any chokepoint disruption has outsized domestic impact. Maritime chokepoint exposure is concentrated across: Suez Canal (moderate dependency, weight 0.20).

Critical import dependencies include crude oil, food commodities, industrial machinery, electricity. Energy import dependency means commodity price spikes and Middle East shipping disruptions transmit directly to domestic inflation and current account pressure.

Primary exports — gold, financial services, agricultural goods — are sensitive to global freight rate movements and trade route disruptions.

SCRI 0.1400 NORMAL — Supply chain channels are operating within baseline parameters. No structural disruption signals active at this time.

Watch signals: Suez Canal closure or capacity restriction → immediate freight rate spike  |  Middle East escalation → energy import disruption and current account deterioration  |  SCFI/WCI/BDI freight index spike above 2-standard-deviation threshold → freight stress trigger

Real Estate Risk Intelligence
Property Price · Mortgage Stress · Commercial RE · Developer Debt · REIT
RERI  0.2000  NORMAL

Banque du Liban is classified as a frontier-market real estate market. Primary stress vector is residential affordability. Commercial real estate exposure is limited CRE concentration risk.

As a frontier market, Lebanon real estate operates with limited pricing transparency, thin transaction volumes, and minimal mortgage market depth. Property rights frameworks may be underdeveloped, creating additional legal risk for international investors.

Developer stress is flagged as active. Developer debt overhang and pre-sale structure failures are live risk vectors — similar to the Evergrande cascade in China. This is the highest-weight RERI driver for Lebanon, pushing the composite score materially above baseline.

RERI 0.2000 NORMAL — Property market stable at structural baseline. No systemic stress indicators active.

Watch signals: Developer bond defaults or pre-sale refund demands → cascade risk  |  Property price index 3-month decline exceeding 2% → KC_RE01 trigger  |  Capital outflow episode → property market freeze and liquidity withdrawal

Insurance Risk Intelligence
Natural Catastrophe · Reinsurance · Property Withdrawal · Financial Guaranty · Climate
INRI  0.3500  WATCH

Banque du Liban faces 2 identified primary natural catastrophe perils: Flood, Earthquake. High-severity events (Earthquake) are present — these carry the highest economic loss potential and are most likely to cause reinsurance market dislocations.

The insurance market in Lebanon is minimal, with negligible domestic insurance infrastructure — catastrophic events rely almost entirely on international reinsurance or sovereign guarantees. Reinsurance profile: high reinsurance dependency — most catastrophic risk ceded offshore.

Climate risk classification: Tier 2 — moderate climate vulnerability with known transition risks. Transition risks (carbon pricing, stranded assets in fossil energy) and moderate physical risks are on the radar. Insurer pricing adjustments are likely within the 3-5 year horizon.

INRI 0.3500 WATCH — Insurance market faces structural vulnerabilities. Nat-cat exposure and/or reinsurance dependency requires monitoring. A major catastrophic event could expose coverage gaps rapidly.

Watch signals: Major Flood event → test of reinsurance capacity and coverage adequacy  |  Global reinsurance market hardening (Jan or July renewals) → premium spike or capacity withdrawal  |  Sovereign disaster declaration without insurance backstop → fiscal cliff  |  Cat bond spread widening → forward signal of reinsurance market stress
